action.reasoning: Observation: The training run crashed with RuntimeError: index_add_(): self (Float) and source (Half) must have the same scalar type. This is caused by torch.cuda.amp.autocast casting the MLP outputs to float16 while the manually created aggregation buffer remains float32. Analysis: We can fix this by removing autocast (safe, plenty of VRAM) or casting msg back to float32 before index_add. Since we need a reliable baseline quickly and have 31GB VRAM, removing autocast is the fastest path to correctness. We also need to ensure train.py and predict.py are complete and consistent. Decision: Rewrite all three files with autocast removed, fix nothing else in the architecture, train for 2 epochs, and submit eval to get the first primary metric score and iteration time.
